title: What is RaveFAM?
content_html: Your rave crew's home base — one place to plan raves together: who's going, the plan, who's driving, where you're staying, and the group chat that pulls it all together.

title: How do I create or join a crew?
content_html: Tap + on the Crews tab to start your own, or ask a crew leader to switch their crew to Recruiting and share their QR code or invite link with you.

title: Can I be in multiple crews?
content_html: Yes — no limit. Join or lead as many crews as you like.

title: What's the difference between Secret, Recruiting, and Locked In?
content_html: Secret is the default — invisible to everyone but you, so you can quietly build your crew list first. Recruiting opens things up: your tribe gets notified and QR/invite links go live so people can claim their spot. Locked In seals the roster — no new members, and your crew gets a heads-up that it's official. You can flip back to Recruiting anytime you need to add someone (no going back to Secret once you leave it).

title: Who can see my crew?
content_html: While Secret, only you. Once Recruiting or Locked In, your crew's existing members can see it — outsiders still need an invite link or QR code to join.

title: How do I leave or remove a crew?
content_html: Open the crew, tap the settings icon, and choose Leave Crew. Crew Leads: leadership transfer is on the way — for now email bump@myravefam.com and we'll sort it.

title: What is the Game Plan tab?
content_html: Once you and a crewmate are both marked as going to the same rave, that rave gets its own Game Plan — a board with Huddle chat, Checklist, Roles, Rides, Stay, and Outfit tabs so logistics live in one place instead of scattered texts.

title: How does Checklist work?
content_html: Add tasks your crew needs done before the rave, grouped by phase — anyone can claim one to own it.

title: How do Roles work?
content_html: Give people a job for the rave — driver, navigator, vibe curator, whatever your crew needs. One person can hold multiple roles, and a role can be shared by more than one person.

title: How does carpool claiming work in Rides?
content_html: Whoever's driving posts their car with an optional seat count; crewmates tap an open seat to claim it. Once every seat's claimed, the ride shows Full. Tap your own claim again to back out.

title: How does Stay work?
content_html: Same idea as Rides but for lodging — post the place you're staying and, if you've got room, open spots for crewmates to claim. Leave the spot count blank if you're just sharing where you'll be, not offering a place.

title: What's Outfit for?
content_html: Coordinate looks and themes with your crew before the rave — drop photo references so everyone knows the vibe.

title: What is Huddle?
content_html: Your crew's real-time group chat — one for the crew overall, plus a dedicated one per rave inside its Game Plan.

title: What's a Beacon?
content_html: An urgent ping that cuts through the chat when it actually matters — lost at the fest, meetup change, whatever. Tap the beacon button inside Huddle to send one.

title: What are PLUR Points?
content_html: Real activity earns you points across Peace, Love, Unity, and Respect — verified by RaveFAM. Tap the PLUR bar on any profile to see exactly what earns points and check your milestones.

title: What is Vendor Village?
content_html: A crew-sourced directory of local vendors — browse what other ravers recommend, save your favorites, and add your own with reviews and badges to help everyone find the good stuff.

title: How do I delete my account?
content_html: Email bump@myravefam.com with subject "Delete my account" — we'll process it within 30 days, including all associated data.

title: I forgot my password — what do I do?
content_html: On the login screen tap Forgot password and enter your email. Check spam if the reset link doesn't show up in a few minutes.

title: A crew leader added me before I signed up — how do I get removed?
content_html: Email bump@myravefam.com with subject "Remove my profile" and we'll delete it within 30 days, no questions asked.

title: Is RaveFAM free to use?
content_html: Yes, fully free to sign up and use, no card required. There's an optional Ko-fi tip jar in the App Guide if you want to support the build.

title: I found a security vulnerability. Who do I tell?
content_html: Email bump@myravefam.com immediately with subject "Security Issue" — we take these seriously and respond fast.

title: How do I turn tips on/off or see them again?
content_html: Open Settings → Privacy & Notifications. Toggle "Tips & hints" off to stop coachmarks from appearing, or tap Reset to see every tip again, including ones you've already dismissed.
